title: Xim's Expansionist Period

'''Xim's Expansionist Period''' is a term used to refer to the first twenty years of [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot's]] reign. It was during this time that he conquered most of the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]], including the [[Livien League]], and the surrounding regions: the [[Thanium sector/Legends|Thanium Worlds]], the [[Far Indrexu]] and the [[Ash Worlds/Legends|Kiirium Reaches]]. This period ended with the [[Xim Wars]].<ref name="Xim Week">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-the-history-of-xim-and-the-tion-cluster|text=Essential Atlas Extra: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster|int=Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster}}</ref>

==Prelude==
In a [[Xer's Campaigns|series of campaigns]], Xim's father [[Xer VIII]] had carved out an expansive empire within the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]] known as the [[Kingdom of Cron]]. However, Xer's expansion had been halted after his defeat by the [[Livien League]] at the [[Battle of Amarin|Battles of Amarin]] and [[Battle of Lorrad|Lorrad]]. Despite this, the Kingdom of Cron's court rituals still referred to the [[Four Thrones of Cron]] - [[Chandaar]], [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]], [[Cadinth]], and the still-independent capital of the League, [[Desevro/Legends|Desevro]].<ref name="Xim Week" />

In [[25,130 BBY]], Xer retired and relinquished the throne to his son [[Xim/Legends|Xim]], who had earned a reputation for brutality and military skill in the [[Cronese Sweeps]]. Xim resolved to complete his father's conquest of the Livien League.<ref name="Xim Week" />

==History==
===The Conquest of the Livien League===
Xim's forces quickly smashed the League at the [[Battle of Jhantoria]]. Xim prepared the raze the League's capital of Desevro as an example to those who would oppose him. However, Xim was dissuaded from this course of action when [[Maslovar Tiatiov]], the military ruler of Desevro, showed him the world's first-class military academies and bureaucracy, explaining to him the Desevrar tradition of training the sons of outlying worlds as [[Janissary/Legends|janissaries]] and civil servants.<ref name="Xim Week" />

Impressed, Xim spared Desevro and proclaimed it the Fourth Throne. The Desevrar philosophy would fuel the later stages of Xim's Expansionist Period. With the Four Thrones under one crown, the Kingdom of Cron was proclaimed [[Xim's empire]].<ref name="Xim Week" />

===The Thanium Worlds===
The second stage of the Expansionist Period was the conquest of the [[Thanium sector/Legends|Thanium Worlds]] from [[25,126 BBY]] to [[25,116 BBY]]. [[Spinward]] of Xim's empire, these planets took their name from the world of [[Thanium]], the Fifth Throne where Xim established his military headquarters. Moving from [[Barseg]] to Thanium, one force moved to conquer [[Stenos/Legends|Maldont]], and from there split to take [[Korriban/Legends|Pesegam]] and [[Thule/Legends|Thule]], while another force took [[Arda system/Legends|Ethain]] and [[Lucazec/Legends|Far Thanium]].<ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

Another force moved from Thanium to take [[Felucia/Legends|Galuch]], [[Mossak]], [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]], [[Draukyze]] and [[Rhen Var/Legends|Rhen Var]]. Finally, a third force from [[Arcan system/Legends|Arcan]] conquered [[Columex/Legends|Nardolin]], [[Belderone/Legends|Vartholium]], [[Trogan/Legends|Ethullum]] and [[Jomark/Legends|Bynas]].<ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

Xim's empire conquered numerous worlds, built new [[Hyperspace beacon/Legends|hyperspace beacons]] for the empire's navigational network, and prepared to settle a wave of Tionese colonists. Species that were considered "worthy" by the Despot, such as the [[Vurk/Legends|Vurk]] and [[Stenax]], were enslaved; others were exterminated. Empty worlds were settled by Tionese colonists, their numbers steadily increasing as part of Xim's strategy of conquest. The Thanium Worlds became the largest and richest of Xim's new provinces.<ref name="Xim Week" />

Xim's spinward push ended at [[Lucazec/Legends|Far Thanium]], [[Troos|Vynx]], and [[Thule/Legends|Thule]], just inside what later generations would call the [[Gordian Reach/Legends|Gordian Reach]]. Beyond Thule was the [[Radama Void/Legends|Radama Void]], an empty expanse that determined the direction of Xim's next conquest.<ref name="Xim Week" />

===The Far Indrexu===
Xim's forces then turned [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|rimward]], moving from [[Kanaver]] to [[Janodral Mizar]], before splitting to conquer [[Makem Te/Legends|Makem Te]] and [[Quermia/Legends|Quermia]], and turning south to [[Florn/Legends|Tinatorn]] and [[Pakuuni/Legends|Pakuuni]]. The second force moved from Janodral Mizar to [[Sembla/Legends|Utavurk]] and [[Yutusk]], before encountering a fierce challenge at the [[Battle of Ranroon]]: the [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] of [[Ranroon]], [[Hylei Indrexu Cortess]], fought Xim's invading janissaries for every inch of ground on her planet. Despite being defeated, Indrexu clearly made a strong impression on Xim, who renamed the Ihala Spiral nebula to the [[Indrexu Spiral]] in her honor, and the [[Far Indrexu]] became the sixth province of Xim's empire, with [[Yutusk]] as the Sixth Throne.<ref name="Xim Week" /><ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

===The Kiirium Reaches===
Concurrent to the war in the [[Thanium sector/Legends|Thanium Worlds]] was the conquest of the [[Ash Worlds/Legends|Kiirium Reaches]] from [[25,120 BBY]] to [[25,096 BBY]]. Rich in the then-valuable military resource [[Kiirium/Legends|kiirium]], the Reaches were a vital addition to Xim's military-industrial complex, and three forces moved to conquer the region. One moved from Pakuuni to [[OHS1782-03 system/Legends|Xoraes]] via [[OHS2132-04 system/Legends|Ulonsus]]. The second moved from [[Caluula/Legends|Caluula]] to [[Dellalt/Legends|Dellalt]], then to [[OHS4140-02 system/Legends|Balshebr]] and [[OHS3842-03|Astigone]]. From there it moved via [[Altratonne|Kveror]] to [[Handooine/Legends|Gwynhes]], where it linked up with the third force sent from [[Murkhana/Legends|Murkhana]] to take [[Wyndigal 2|Huronom]] and [[Sy Myrth/Legends|Far Barseg]]. Both forces then moved to conquer [[Jabiim/Legends|Gwynhes Minor]]. Huronom and Astigone then became the Seventh and Eighth Thrones of the empire.<ref name="Xim Week" /><ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

==Aftermath==
It was after the conquest of the Kiirium Reaches that Xim first learned of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] after encountering [[Sakiyan/Legends|Sakiyan]] and [[Nimbanel/Legends|Nimbanel]] traders. Knowing from these stories that the [[Hutt Empire]] could not be conquered outright, he hoped to coexist with them while at the same time preparing for war. Diplomatic contact between the Hutts and the Tion broke down, however, beginning the [[Xim Wars|Hutt–Xim conflict]] in [[25,102 BBY]] that would see the end of Xim's empire.<ref name="Xim Week" /><ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

[[Hylei Indrexu Cortess]] was a debated historical figure for millennia after Xim's wars. According to the later [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] dramatist [[Direus'pei]] in his play [[Evocar (play)|''Evocar'']], Xim murdered his father after discovering that he had taken Indrexu as his wife. Other scholars considered this a pure literary invention, with some totally dismissing Indrexu's existence. Still other scholars accepted her existence but argued about her role in Xim's empire and her ultimate fate. To some she was a minor figure, a fixture at Xim's court whose name endured; to others she was the pivot around which the empire turned, and even the very cause of the Cronese Wars, Xim's expansion and everything thereafter.<ref name="Xim Week" />

==Xim's military==
Xim the Despot's military was extremely advanced for its day, built from lost [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] technology. The scale of his armies and conquests meant that numerous examples of Xim's weaponry survived to the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] era. Xim's [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] were divided into several castes. Most were [[Janissary/Legends|janissaries]] taken from occupied worlds, trained from childhood to serve as soldiers. They wore [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and helmets of lightweight ceramic alloy and carried either [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrowers]] or [[heatbeam]]s, which used [[Laser/Legends|lasers]] to generate jets of [[Plasma/Legends|plasma]]. Heatbeamers wore tough, fireproof padded gloves and [[Kama/Legends|''kamas'']] as protection against their weapons' superheated exhausts. The ''kama'' would later be adopted by the [[Thyrsus/Legends|Thyrsus]] [[Sun Guard]] and by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] people as part of their culture.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

Among Xim's elite units were the [[Duinarbulon Star Lancers]], who wore gloss-black ceramic armor and carried advanced (for the time) [[Beam tube/Legends|beam tubes]], distant ancestors of the [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. The infantry was supported by tracked [[Tank/Legends|tanks]], armored [[Groundcar/Legends|groundcars]], and [[floater]]s armed with beam tube cannons. Elite vehicles were armored with [[Kiirium/Legends|kiirium]] to reflect energy weapons. Xim's most famous soldiers were the [[war-robot]]s, three-meter tall [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] armed with heatbeams, particle dischargers, and [[pulse-wave cannon]]s. The war-robots were typically kept in reserve for use as shock troops.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

Xim's [[Warship/Legends|warships]] were smaller than [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]-era craft and were all unique designs, since the ancient Tionese saw warship construction as an art form as opposed to an industry. Under the [[Anaxes War College System/Legends|Anaxes War College System]], the bulk of Xim's warships would be considered [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]] or [[Corvette/Legends|corvettes]], and included [[Argaian hemiolia]], [[Livien cutter]]s, [[Thanium star-glaive]]s, and [[Cronian battlebird/Legends|Cronian battlebirds]] in mixed squadrons. Larger vessels, around the size of a [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], included [[Cronese harpice]]s, [[Brigian penteconter]]s, [[Thanium polyreme]]s, [[Yutuski rakehell]]s and [[Xolochi Dreadnought]]s. Xim's [[Flagship/Legends|flagships]] included the ''[[Eibon Scimitar]]'' and the ''[[Deathknell]]''.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

Xim's naval weapons were primarily [[Torpedo/Legends|torpedos]], [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]], and [[Pulse cannon/Legends|pulse cannons]], which melted enemy hulls and disrupted circuitry with blasts of radiation. The Despot's ships were armored with [[Kiirium/Legends|kiirium]], a superconducting alloy that gave each ship a mirrored hull that could reflect enemy energy weapons. [[Mytag crystal/Legends|Mytag crystals]] were used in communications and sensor systems to protect them from electromagnetic attack. [[Bordhell-type fuel slug|Bordhell]] fuel slugs gave Xim's warships unparalleled range and speed.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>
